question.text: Under Section 5 of PMLA, who has the authority to order provisional attachment of property involved in money laundering?

answer.text: Any police officer of Inspector rank or above
The Director or any officer not below the rank of Deputy Director authorized by the Director
The Adjudicating Authority appointed under Section 6
Any officer of SEBI not below the rank of General Manager

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Section 5, the Director (of Enforcement) or any officer not below the rank of Deputy Director authorized by the Director can order provisional attachment. The attachment must be based on a reason to believe (recorded in writing) that proceeds of crime are likely to be concealed, transferred, or dealt with.
**The Trap:** The Adjudicating Authority (Section 6) confirms the attachment after the provisional period — it does not initiate it. SEBI officers have no direct power under Section 5. Regular police do not handle PMLA matters.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The hierarchy under PMLA involves Director (ED), Deputy Director, Assistant Director — all appointed under Section 49. The Adjudicating Authority is a separate quasi-judicial body under Section 6.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The officer must record "reason to believe" in writing before attachment. This is a safeguard that prevents arbitrary action. As an intermediary, if you receive an attachment order, verify it is from an authorized officer.
